current.summary: Executed all 44 CDR beads (phases 1-16), real-world tested against 8 repos, created 5 ADRs, discovered content-based classification gap requiring design pass.

activeWork.description: Content discovery redesign: execution complete, gap analysis for content-based classification needed
activeWork.progress: All 44 beads executed and closed. New analyzer package: 20 files, ~3,000 lines, 88.6% coverage. 11 detectors, full pipeline (Walk → Match → Classify → Dedup → References → Partition → Manifest → Validate). CLI command `syllago manifest generate` working. Analyzer wired into registry-add. 5 ADRs created. Real-world testing found gap: repos with non-standard structures (PAI, BMAD) get zero detection.

recentDecisions: All 44 CDR beads executed in single session — full analyzer package complete
5 ADRs created: hub-and-spoke architecture, manifest-first path, executable always confirms, provider priority tiebreaking, empty manifest authoritative
Real-world testing revealed content-based classification gap — repos with non-standard structures (PAI Packs/, BMAD src/bmm/agents/) get zero detection
Gap is architectural: MatchPatterns step is only entry point to pipeline, all patterns are provider-specific, no content-inspection fallback exists
README.md files in agents/ and commands/ dirs are false positives — worth filtering
Dedup working correctly in practice (kitchen-sink greeting skill aliased across CC + Gemini)
nextSteps: Design pass for content-based classification (bead syllago-0byne) — read gap doc at docs/plans/2026-04-01-content-based-classification-gap.md
Decide: content-signal detector vs user-directed discovery vs quick-win pattern additions vs combination
Fix README.md false positives in TopLevelDetector (agents/README.md classified as agent)
Pre-existing test failures: TestDoctorCheckRegistries_WithConfig, TestGenproviders_* (3 tests, not CDR-related)

context.notes: The CDR implementation is functionally complete — all plan phases executed, all tests passing, 88.6% coverage. The gap discovered during real-world testing is a design-level issue, not a bug. The current architecture optimizes for precision (known provider patterns) over recall (finding all AI content). PAI uses Packs/pai-*-skill/ and BMAD uses src/bmm/agents/*.agent.yaml — both completely custom structures that no pattern-based approach would catch without either dedicated detectors or a fundamentally different classification approach. The gap doc has 5 design questions and a quick-wins table. Uncommitted files in git status are from other sessions (agent-skills-spec work), not CDR.

previousSessions.summary: Designed and planned the content discovery redesign — 45 design decisions, 3 panel reviews, 44 beads created
